name: Candied Citrus Peel
recipeIngredient: 4 oranges, or 6 lemons or limes, or 2 grapefruits
2 cups granulated sugar (divided), plus extra for layering stored peel
2 tablespoons light corn syrup or 1/4 teaspoon cream of tartar
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the fruit thoroughly to remove any possible chemical residue.
Use a sharp paring knife to quarter the oranges or grapefruit, then remove the quarter-segments of peel. For lemons and limes, slice off wide strips of peel, including the pith, cutting right down to the fruit. Aim for about 1/8 inch thick peel; thicker is acceptable. You should have approximately 2 cups of loosely packed peel.
2. Place the peel in a medium heavy-bottomed nonreactive saucepan, add cold water to cover, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer gently for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let the peel stand in hot water for 15 minutes. Drain in a colander and rinse under very cold water; the peel should feel soft.
3. Gently scrape off and discard the soft white pith inside each segment of peel using a teaspoon. Slice the peel into strips about 1/4 inch wide.
4. Prepare the syrup by combining 1 cup of sugar, 1/2 cup water, and light corn syrup or cream of tartar in the same saucepan. Stir to dissolve the sugar, then bring to a boil, swirling the pan several times. Use a pastry brush dipped in cold water to clean the sides of the pan.
5. Boil the syrup until it becomes clear, then add the prepared peel and immerse it in the syrup. If using a candy thermometer, attach it to the pan. Cook gently over moderate heat for about 30 minutes, or until the syrup reaches 230°F (a small amount dropped into ice water should form a thread; just before the soft-ball stage) and most of the syrup has been absorbed. Remove from heat.
6. Transfer the peel using a slotted spoon to a bowl or pan and toss well to coat. Spread the peel on wax paper-covered baking sheet or wire rack over wax paper to air-dry.
7. After several hours, or overnight, the peel should be crisp outside but flexible and chewy inside. If too soft, it was not cooked long enough in the syrup at a high enough temperature; if overly crisp when bent, it may have been cooked at too high a temperature but will still taste good. Store the peel between layers of sugar in an airtight container. Yield: about 2 cups candied peel
